We transform AI Experience from a one-time implementation
into a system rooted in your organization.
intro.box5: From diagnosis to adoption — we stay with you every step of the way.

framework.subtitle: A 4-phase execution framework for sustainable AI transformation

step1.tagline: We look at your business first, not the tools.

step1.expandedSubtitle: We look at your business first, not the tools.

step2.tagline: No two companies share the same AX roadmap.

We don't build it for you.
We build it together so your team can build on their own.

step4.subtitle: Adoption & Scale

part3.subtitle2: We take responsibility for systems that actually work in practice.

testimonial1.title: The balance between theory and practice was perfect
testimonial1.subtitle: Training I could apply to my job immediately
testimonial1.quote: I took the Figma Site training — I knew about it but had never used it, so it was really valuable to learn. NotebookLM was new to me, and I can see it being very useful when picking up new tools. It was great to learn things I didn't know despite working in design.

testimonial2.title: Applied N8N automation to my work immediately
testimonial2.subtitle: I could learn even complex tools step by step
testimonial2.quote: N8N is more complex than other AI tools, but being able to learn from initial setup step by step was very helpful. The training went beyond simple usage to how to apply it to actual work, which was a great help.

testimonial3.title: My approach to prompts changed completely
testimonial3.subtitle: The quality of my work outputs improved along with it
testimonial3.quote: As my understanding of effective questioning improved, my overall work utilization and quality of outputs improved together. Learning prompt techniques by type meant I could apply them directly to real work.

testimonial4.title: Team-customized training meant immediate application
testimonial4.subtitle: Content that fit perfectly with our team's work
testimonial4.quote: Running it at the team level rather than group training meant I could ask questions as they came up. Working through the challenges we actually face in our work meant we could learn concrete action plans rather than just suggestions.

edu.headline:
NextGen AI's corporate training
is "training that changes how you work."
edu.subtext:
We don't do training that ends with theory.
Redesign your work with real AI Thinking,
and automate repetitive tasks with AI to drive results.

flipCard.title: Your inquiry has been received!
flipCard.description:
An AI expert will be in touch shortly.
We'll prepare a customized curriculum for you.

testimonial1.quote: Vague AI adoption became concrete guidelines for our company.
testimonial1.description: Before the training, I knew about AI but had no idea how to embed it in actual work. Through NextGenAI's training, we were able to create usage guidelines tailored to each department.
testimonial1.author: HR Manager at Financial Company D

testimonial2.quote: Not only do they create customized curricula, but the detailed learning coaching and management is a huge draw.
testimonial2.description: Starting with online training for all employees, we expanded to holding company staff and now to all affiliates.
testimonial2.author: HR Head at Large Corporate Holding Company

testimonial3.quote: It was only a month and a half from our first meeting to pulling off an event this big — thanks to your active support, effort, and capability.
testimonial3.description: You were instrumental in making this hackathon a success. The excellent specialist coaches, special lecture speakers, thorough materials preparation, and quick work from the operations team were all impressive.
testimonial3.author: Doo-Hwan Kim, POSCO Human Resource Development Institute

testimonial4.quote: After AI training, the team's overall work efficiency improved noticeably. Repetitive tasks were reduced by over 50%.
testimonial4.description: Rather than just teaching tool usage, designing the actual workflows we could apply to our work meant we saw results immediately after training.
testimonial4.author: Operations Team Lead at E-commerce Company M

testimonial5.quote: From executives to practitioners, we now have a common understanding and language around AI.
testimonial5.description: Company-wide AI literacy training smoothed inter-departmental communication and sped up alignment on AI projects. It became a catalyst for changing our organizational culture.
testimonial5.author: DX Promotion Team Lead at Manufacturing Company S

painpoint1.question: Worried about security incidents?
painpoint1.description: We simultaneously build enterprise data security and AI ethics frameworks.

painpoint2.question: Does each department need different capabilities?
painpoint2.description: We provide curricula optimized for marketing, sales, HR, development, and more.

painpoint3.question: Hard to measure training effectiveness?
painpoint3.description: We quantify and visualize capability changes before and after training across 6 metrics.

problem1.title: Don't know where to start
problem1.subtitle: No clear AI adoption direction
problem1.quote: We're using ChatGPT... but I don't know how to apply it to our actual work.
problem1.detail1: Tools exist but aren't connected to workflows
problem1.detail2: Can't scale from individual use to team/organizational use

problem2.title: Using tools at only 30% capacity
problem2.subtitle: Lots of features but only copy-paste level usage
problem2.quote: We use GPT, Gemini... but just throw questions at it and copy-paste.
problem2.detail1: Not properly using prompt principles, GPTs, automation features
problem2.detail2: Used only as a 'search replacement' rather than a productivity tool

problem3.title: Feeding data to AI is the hardest part
problem3.subtitle: Don't know how to delegate work to AI
problem3.quote: We have lots of materials... but how do we feed them so AI can do the work?
problem3.detail1: Don't know how to connect documents, Excel, Notion, manuals
problem3.detail2: Humans end up reorganizing everything again

problem4.title: Can't move from individual to organizational use
problem4.subtitle: Everyone uses it individually, nothing is shared, nothing becomes a system
problem4.quote: A few people use it well... but overall team productivity is the same.
problem4.detail2: Not structurally embedded in work processes

problem5.title: Results aren't visible, so momentum fades
problem5.subtitle: Can't prove the effect of training/adoption
problem5.quote: We've adopted AI... but so what has actually improved by how much?
problem5.detail1: ROI, time savings, throughput improvements aren't measured
problem5.detail2: Ends up disappearing like a trend

problemCommon.label: The common cause of these problems
problemCommon.before: Learning AI as a 'tool' without changing the '

program1.title: AI Thinking Process
program1.subtitle: Redesign work in a way that lets you delegate to AI
program1.description:
Rather than bringing existing problems as-is,
we redefine how each task can be delegated to AI.
program1.detail1: What data needs to be input and how
program1.detail2: What AI can do and what humans still need to do
program1.conclusion:
We establish these criteria first,
then design actual working workflows and scenarios with AI.

program2.title: Role-Specific Practical Work
program2.subtitle: Automate different work across departments in their own ways
program2.description:
For sales, marketing, management support, and more —
we build AI workflows that match actual work processes.
program2.detail1: Sales: Customer data analysis and automated proposal generation
program2.detail2: Marketing: From content planning to performance analysis
program2.detail3: Admin: Automated reports, meeting minutes, schedule management
program2.conclusion:
We design practical automation scenarios
directly tied to each department's KPIs.

program3.title: AI Governance & Security
program3.subtitle: Set the 'enterprise standard' for AI usage
program3.description:
Beyond just using tools,
we build a framework for safe and sustainable usage.
program3.detail2: AI usage ethics framework and policy design
program3.conclusion:
We provide a governance framework
for sustainable AI adoption.

process1.title: Pre-Diagnosis
process1.subtitle: AI-Q Capability Assessment
process1.description:
We don't start with training.
We first diagnose the team and organization's AI readiness level,
then clarify where and how things need to change.

process2.title: Strategy Development
process2.subtitle: Customized Curriculum Design
process2.description:
Based on diagnostic results,
we design the most effective training strategy and curriculum for your organization.
Completely customized by department, role, and objective.

process3.title: Customized Training
process3.subtitle: Role- and Level-Specific Practical Training
process3.description:
Not generic lectures —
practice-focused training applied directly to each person's actual work.
We build actual automations and work structures during the training.

process4.title: Post-Evaluation
process4.subtitle: Performance Analysis & Governance Guide
process4.description:
After training ends,
we analyze capability changes and business outcomes in numbers,
and provide AI usage guides and frameworks for ongoing operation.

thinking.title: NextGenAI Thinking Process
thinking.subtitle1: Most AI projects fail not because of technology.
thinking.subtitle2: "Because they start without defining what to delegate to AI."
thinking.philosophy1: Competitive advantage in the AI era is not
thinking.philosophy3: but the ability to decide what to delegate to AI

header.line1: We start by dividing work into
header.desc2: Some work gets automated, some is AI-collaborated,
header.desc3: and some still needs to be done by humans.
header.desc4: This distinction determines the success or failure of AX.

classification.title: Rather than 'what to automate,'
classification.subtitle: we define 'what NOT to automate'

card1.title: Human Domain
card1.desc: Strategy and judgment remain in the human domain.
card1.detail: Negotiation, strategy development, performance evaluation — areas requiring sophisticated judgment

card2.title: AI Collaboration
card2.desc: AI creates drafts, humans make final decisions.
card2.detail: Reports, proposal drafts, customer service — AI Agent + approval workflow

card3.title: Efficiency
card3.desc: Not frequent, but handle annoying tasks fast.
card3.detail: Form filling, data organization — button-based automated draft generation

card4.title: Full Automation
card4.desc: Repetitive, rule-based tasks — handled by the system.
card4.detail: Notifications, data collection, report generation — n8n + schedule-based full automation

guide.title: This is how we classify your team's work
guide.desc: Look at just these two factors to immediately know where each task belongs.

classification.bottom3: we redesign the nature of work first.
